headline: Stagflation playbook in full effect as geopolitical risk premium drives energy while Fed maintains hawkish stance despite growth concerns.
situation: We're witnessing a textbook stagflation scenario with oil above $110 from Iran tensions, Fed refusing to pivot at 3.75% despite recession fears, and trade wars compounding supply-side inflation. Energy and commodities have massively outperformed while growth stocks languish, suggesting markets are pricing persistent inflation over growth concerns.
key_tension: Fed faces impossible choice between fighting energy-driven inflation and supporting an economy under pressure from multiple geopolitical shocks.

bull_case.scenario: Iran tensions de-escalate, oil falls back below $90, allowing Fed to shift focus to growth support
bull_case.trigger: Diplomatic breakthrough on Iran or oil inventory release driving WTI below $85

bear_case.scenario: Strait of Hormuz closure or broader Middle East conflict sends oil to $150+ while Fed maintains restrictive policy
bear_case.trigger: Iran blocks Hormuz shipping lanes or attacks Saudi facilities

prudent_strategy: Overweight the stagflation trade that's already working - energy and real assets that benefit from supply disruptions and persistent inflation. Underweight duration and growth stocks until geopolitical risks subside or Fed pivots. The regime change is clear but timing the exit remains impossible.

cpi.what_to_watch: Energy component will dominate given $110+ oil - core services matter less when headline inflation is geopolitically driven
cpi.high: Add to XLE and DBC positions, short TLT on expectations Fed stays hawkish longer
cpi.low: Trim energy overweight cautiously - could be temporary base effect with geopolitical premium intact
cpi.inline: Maintains current stagflation thesis, no major position changes needed

fomc.what_to_watch: Whether Powell acknowledges stagflation risk or maintains singular inflation focus despite growth headwinds
fomc.hold: Validates current positioning - energy/commodities continue outperforming growth
fomc.hike: Major stagflation signal - aggressively add XLE, sell QQQ, buy more GLD
fomc.cut: Pivot moment - rotate from energy into oversold growth names like MSFT/NVDA
fomc.emergency_cut: Crisis response - major geopolitical escalation likely, buy defense stocks and gold

filings.what_to_watch: Whether smart money rotated into energy/commodities in Q1 or remained overweight growth despite the regime shift
filings.bullish_signal: Major funds increased XLE/DBC positions while reducing QQQ/growth exposure in Q1
filings.bearish_signal: Institutional investors still overweight tech despite -17% to -33% drawdowns, suggesting more forced selling ahead
